assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Regular channel clusters will be force-upgraded to 1.31 when 1.30 reaches EoS, with both control plane and node pools automatically upgraded

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states Extended channel clusters have 'up to 24 months total (vs 14 months standard)' support and 'No forced upgrade' with flexibility to stay on 1.30 until ready

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters face systematic node-level EoS enforcement — nodes on EoS versions are force-upgraded
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Systematic node-level EoS enforcement applies from 1.32 onward' for No channel clusters and that they will be force-upgraded as part of legacy cleanup

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance exclusions but explicitly states 'Even no upgrades exclusions are bypassed at EoS' - contradicts the expectation about deferring EoS force-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'Proactive Manual Upgrade' as Option 1 and states 'Upgrade before EoS hits to maintain control over timing and sequencing'

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response includes a section on monitoring with Cloud Logging notifications: 'Enable 72-hour advance notifications (Configure in Cloud Logging, filter for GKE upgrade notifications)'

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking for deprecated APIs like 'v1beta1 CronJob usage' but does not specifically mention the deprecation insights dashboard as a tool

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response does not mention 'snowflaking' or warn against manually freezing versions, nor does it discuss the security and reliability risks of this practice
